page: Amazon Web Services i7i.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, storage-optimized server instance featuring Intel Xeon 8559C processors. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it delivers 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 768 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. For local storage, the instance includes 22,500 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, paired with a baseline network bandwidth of 56 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core and memory performance. The instance achieves top-tier status in Passmark CPU tests, including extended instructions, encryption, and physics evaluations. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and gzip compression also register in the top tier. In database-specific benchmarks, the server demonstrates top-tier performance in Passmark database operations and Redis SET operations, processing 122.39 million operations per second. Memory bandwidth tests show strong read and write capabilities, supported by a 320 MB L3 cache and advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX.

Given its bare-metal architecture and massive local NVMe SSD storage, the i7i.metal-24xl offers high resource density for I/O-intensive workloads. The combination of dedicated compute resources, DDR5 memory, and direct-attached storage makes this instance highly suitable for large-scale relational and non-relational databases, data warehousing, and high-throughput transactional applications. It is also well-suited for software compilation, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks where local storage speed and memory capacity are critical.
description: The Amazon Web Services i7i.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, storage-optimized server designed for high-throughput, data-intensive workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8559C processor with 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores, the instance features 768 GB of DDR5 memory and 22,500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It operates on the x86_64 architecture with a baseline network bandwidth of 56 Gbps. Benchmark data shows top-tier performance in database operations, Redis SET operations, and multi-threaded decompression, alongside strong memory bandwidth. The hardware configuration is optimized for large-scale databases, data warehousing, and high-performance file systems, offering high resource density and direct-attached storage without virtualization overhead.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7i.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, storage-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs, 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 22,500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-throughput database workloads.

tagline: A bare-metal, storage-optimized instance combining ninety-six vCPUs, DDR5 memory, and twenty-two terabytes of local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: Ninety-six vCPUs and forty-eight physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8559C processors
768 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz
22,500 GB of direct-attached local NVMe SSD storage
56 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer
Top-tier performance in database operations and multi-threaded decompression
Optimized for large-scale databases, data warehousing, and transactional workloads
